details.body: The service-level `update_task_run` function only rewrites the NDJSON lineage file but does NOT update the MilestoneSnapshot (progress counters: in_progress_beads, completed_beads, failed_beads; active_bead). By contrast, `record_bead_completion` correctly updates both. If a controller calls `update_task_run` to transition a Running entry to Succeeded/Failed, the snapshot's progress counters and active_bead will become permanently stale. `update_task_run` must also accept `snapshot_store` and `journal_store` parameters and apply the same snapshot mutations and journal event emission that `record_bead_completion` does.

In `record_bead_completion` (service.rs lines 372-376), any non-Succeeded outcome—including `TaskRunOutcome::Skipped`—emits a `MilestoneEventType::BeadFailed` journal event. Skipped should either emit a distinct event type or at minimum not be recorded as a failure. Currently the progress counter also doesn't increment for Skipped (it falls through the match arms), so the total bead accounting is also off when Skipped is used.

The relationship between `record_bead_completion` (append-based) and `update_task_run` (in-place-rewrite) is undefined and untested in combination. `record_bead_completion` appends a NEW completion entry alongside the existing Running entry, while `update_task_run` mutates the existing Running entry in place. If both are used for the same bead, the NDJSON file will have an inconsistent mix of append-style and update-style entries. The code should document which one the controller should use and when, and add a test covering what happens if both are called on the same run.

In the FS adapter, `update_task_run` performs a non-atomic read-modify-write: it reads the entire NDJSON file, deserializes all entries, mutates one, and rewrites the whole file. Between the read and the write, a concurrent `append_task_run` call (e.g., from another process recording a different bead) could add lines that will be silently dropped by the rewrite. At minimum, add a comment documenting this limitation; ideally, use file locking (e.g., `flock`) around the read-modify-write cycle.

`src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:324-350` still records completion by appending a second `TaskRunEntry`, while the new `update_task_run` helper at `src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:419-437` only mutates `task-runs.ndjson` and does not update the snapshot or journal. That leaves no correct completion path: using `record_bead_completion` duplicates every attempt in lineage, but using `update_task_run` alone leaves `active_bead`, progress counters, and journal events stale. Please consolidate completion into one service flow that updates snapshot/journal and mutates the existing task-run row instead of appending another row.

`src/adapters/fs.rs:2340-2350` updates the last row matching only `bead_id` + `project_id`, even though this change adds `run_id` to `TaskRunEntry` for per-run auditability. If one Ralph project produces multiple runs for the same bead, `update_task_run` can silently rewrite the wrong attempt. The lookup should use `run_id` when present, or reject ambiguous matches instead of mutating the last matching row.

In `FsTaskRunLineageStore::update_task_run` (fs.rs ~line 2471-2479), the `is_terminal()` guard rejects updates to already-finalized entries. However, when `run_id` is provided and matches an exact entry at line ~2407, the code reaches this guard *after* selecting the target. If the exact-match entry is terminal, the error message says 'already finalized' but doesn't include the run_id in the message, making it harder to debug. Add `run={run_id}` to the error details string for the terminal-rejection branch, consistent with the other error messages in this function.

The service-layer `update_task_run` function (service.rs ~line 390-472) performs three sequential I/O operations without a shared lock: (1) updates the NDJSON file via the port (which holds its own lock), (2) reads+writes the snapshot, (3) appends to the journal. If the process crashes after step 1 but before step 2, the lineage file will show 'succeeded' but the snapshot's `in_progress_beads` counter will remain stale and `completed_beads` won't be incremented. Consider documenting this partial-failure mode, or add a reconciliation/repair function that can reconstruct snapshot counters from the lineage NDJSON as the source of truth.

`find_runs_for_bead` is not actually backward-compatible with existing `task-runs.ndjson` history. In the pre-change format, each attempt was persisted as two rows (a `running` start row and a terminal completion row). The new query at `src/adapters/fs.rs:2500`-`src/adapters/fs.rs:2516` only filters by `bead_id` and sorts the raw rows, so a historical attempt is returned twice and can still appear as an in-flight `running` row forever. That breaks the acceptance criterion for answering “what happened to bead X?” from existing milestone state. The query needs to collapse legacy start/completion pairs into a single attempt record before returning results, and there should be a regression test covering a legacy NDJSON file with paired rows.

`update_task_run` can fail on legacy data when `run_id` is unavailable, even though `run_id` is explicitly optional. The matching logic in `src/adapters/fs.rs:2387`-`src/adapters/fs.rs:2468` treats every non-terminal row as an open attempt. For pre-change history, completed attempts leave behind their original `running` start rows, so a retry in the same `project_id` can easily produce multiple open matches and trip the new `provide run_id to disambiguate` error path. That means older milestones cannot reliably finalize new attempts unless they happen to have a `run_id`. The updater should recognize and ignore legacy start rows that already have a matching terminal row, or otherwise normalize legacy history before ambiguity checks. Add a test that seeds old-format rows and verifies a no-`run_id` retry can still be finalized.

`src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:417-474` seals the lineage row before updating the snapshot or journal, but `src/adapters/fs.rs:2545-2556` rejects any later finalize on an already-terminal entry. If `write_snapshot` or `append_event` fails after the row update, retrying `record_bead_completion`/`update_task_run` can no longer repair the snapshot/journal drift, so a transient I/O failure leaves milestone state permanently inconsistent unless `task-runs.ndjson` is edited manually. Make completion idempotent for the same terminal payload, or defer sealing the lineage row until the rest of the milestone state is durable.

`src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:305-308` moves a started bead into `MilestoneStatus::Active`, but `src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:448-456` clears `active_bead` on completion without changing `status` or re-validating. `src/contexts/milestone_record/model.rs:172-176` explicitly defines `status == Active && active_bead == None` as inconsistent, so every normal completion now persists a snapshot that violates the model invariant. Completion needs to transition out of `Active` (or otherwise preserve the invariant) before writing the snapshot.

In `update_task_run` (service.rs:590), `snapshot.updated_at` is set to `finalized_run.finished_at.unwrap_or(finished_at)`, which on a replay uses the ORIGINAL completion timestamp, not the current wall clock. If any other operation updated the snapshot between the failed attempt and the retry, `updated_at` would regress to an earlier value. Fix: use `snapshot.updated_at.max(finished_at)` or take `max(finalized_run.finished_at.unwrap_or(finished_at), snapshot.updated_at)` so the timestamp never moves backward.

`normalize_task_runs` + `task_runs_same_attempt` + `merge_task_run_attempt` in service.rs (lines 134-191) duplicate `collapse_runs_for_bead` + `same_attempt` + `merge_attempt_rows` in fs.rs (lines 2301-2407). The only difference is that `collapse_runs_for_bead` also filters by bead_id. Extract the shared merge/normalize logic into a single location (e.g., a function on TaskRunEntry or a helper module) and have both call sites use it, to prevent the two copies from silently diverging.

`update_task_run()` no longer replays completions against legacy two-row NDJSON attempts, which breaks the backward-compatibility/repair story the new service API promises. `service.rs` says repeated terminal completions should be able to repair snapshot/journal state without duplication (`src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:545-548`), but the no-`run_id` selector in `FsTaskRunLineageStore::update_task_run()` only accepts a single non-terminal match and explicitly filters out superseded legacy start rows (`src/adapters/fs.rs:2557-2577`). For existing data written by the old code, one attempt is stored as `running` + terminal rows with the same `bead_id`/`project_id` and no `run_id`, so replaying that completion now errors with "ambiguous task run update ... provide run_id" instead of repairing the milestone state.

`record_bead_start()` is not idempotent for the same `(bead_id, project_id, run_id)`, and that can strand a run permanently once `update_task_run()` starts requiring a unique row. The start path appends a new running entry before writing snapshot/journal and allows the same bead to be started again (`src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:434-472`). If the first start call fails after `append_task_run()` and the controller retries, the file now contains duplicate running rows with the same `run_id`; later, `FsTaskRunLineageStore::update_task_run()` treats multiple exact `run_id` matches as ambiguous and refuses to finalize either one (`src/adapters/fs.rs:2510-2555`). With the new in-place finalization model, a transient start-side failure can therefore make the bead impossible to complete.

`src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:176-189` only converts an idle milestone back to `ready` when the old snapshot status was already `active`. `update_task_run()` relies on that helper after sealing the lineage row (`src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:601-610`), while `record_bead_start()` still appends the running lineage entry before writing `status.json` (`src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:458-480`). If the start path fails after the append but before the snapshot write, a later completion repair fixes the counters but leaves the milestone stuck in `planning` (or any other stale non-terminal status) even though a bead already ran and finished. Please make reconciliation derive the correct idle status from lineage for these partial-failure repairs, not only for the `active -> ready` case.

`record_bead_start()` is still not concurrency-safe for the idempotent retry case. It decides whether a running entry already exists from an unlocked `read_task_runs()` snapshot (`src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:387-423`) and only later performs a blind append (`src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:458-470`, `src/adapters/fs.rs:2428-2437`). The `BeadStarted` journal dedupe is another unlocked read-then-append (`src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:482-488`). Two controllers retrying the same `(bead_id, project_id, run_id)` concurrently can both observe “no existing row”, both append a running entry, and both emit start events. `update_task_run()` can collapse duplicate lineage rows later, but the duplicate journal history is permanent and snapshot counts are wrong until completion. The compare-and-insert for start idempotence needs to happen under a single lock.

The default trait implementation of `record_task_run_start` (service.rs:96-155) performs a read-check-append sequence without any locking, creating a TOCTOU race if any future `TaskRunLineagePort` implementor relies on the default. The FS override is correctly locked, but the default is misleading — it provides safety-critical invariant checks (active bead exclusivity, finalized-run rejection) that cannot actually be enforced without atomicity. Either (a) remove the default implementation and make `record_task_run_start` a required method, forcing implementors to provide their own atomic version, or (b) document prominently on the trait method that the default is NOT concurrency-safe and must be overridden by any production adapter.

Same TOCTOU issue exists in the default trait implementation of `append_event_if_missing` (service.rs:65-79): it calls `read_journal()` then `append_event()` as two separate non-atomic operations. The FS override correctly holds the journal lock across both. The default should either be removed or marked unsafe for concurrent use.

In `record_bead_start` (service.rs:462-496), the snapshot read-reconcile-write cycle is not protected by any lock. Two concurrent callers can both read the snapshot, both succeed at `record_task_run_start` (which is idempotent under the FS lock), but then both write the snapshot in a last-writer-wins race. While the content is semantically equivalent for the idempotent start case, this pattern is fragile: if the first caller's reconcile + write is interleaved with a different operation (e.g., `update_task_run` completing the bead), the second caller's stale snapshot write could overwrite the completion state. Consider either (a) adding a snapshot lock to `record_bead_start` / `update_task_run`, or (b) documenting that callers must externally serialize milestone-level operations.

src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:463-487, src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:250-258, and src/adapters/fs.rs:2526-2602: `record_bead_start` writes a running lineage row before it proves the milestone can legally leave a terminal state. Starting a bead on a `completed` or `abandoned` milestone appends `task-runs.ndjson`, then the second reconcile fails because terminal snapshots cannot have an active bead. The call returns an error after mutating lineage, so later start attempts fail until some out-of-band finalize/manual cleanup removes that stray running row. Reject terminal milestones before `record_task_run_start`, or make the lineage and snapshot update atomic.

src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:470-487 and 593-614 together with src/adapters/fs.rs:2536-2602 and 2617-2832: the milestone snapshot is recomputed and written outside the task-run lock. That leaves a lost-update window where one thread can read lineage, another thread can start or finalize a different bead, and the first thread then overwrites `status.json` with stale `active_bead` and progress counters. Example: A finalizes bead-1 and computes `Ready`; B starts bead-2 and persists `Active`; A then writes `Ready`, hiding the active run even though `task-runs.ndjson` says bead-2 is still running. The lineage file is protected, but the snapshot is not, so milestone state is still not concurrency-safe.

The concurrency test `start_and_completion_serialize_milestone_snapshot_updates` uses `recv_timeout(Duration::from_millis(100))` to assert thread 2 is blocked by the write lock. Under heavy CI load, thread 2 may not have been scheduled yet, making the assertion pass vacuously without proving the lock blocks. Consider using a synchronization barrier or a second entered_tx channel from thread 2 that fires before the lock attempt, so the test can confirm thread 2 actually reached the lock contention point before checking it hasn't completed.

In `persist_plan`, `plan_hash.clone()` inside the closure is an unnecessary allocation. The closure is `FnOnce` so it could capture `plan_hash: String` by value (add `move` keyword or restructure) and use `Some(plan_hash)` directly. Same pattern exists with `outcome_detail.clone()` in `update_task_run`. Not a correctness issue but a needless copy on every call.

`src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:168-199` recomputes `MilestoneProgress` by counting every lineage row after `collapse_task_run_attempts`, but `collapse_task_run_attempts` in `src/contexts/milestone_record/model.rs:399-430` only merges legacy start/completion pairs, not separate retries. Since `MilestoneProgress` is documented as bead counts in `src/contexts/milestone_record/model.rs:191-205`, a bead that fails once and then succeeds on retry will persist `failed_beads=1` and `completed_beads=1` for that one bead. That makes `status.json` report historical attempts as current milestone state. The aggregation needs to collapse retries to one current state per bead, or store attempt-history counters separately from bead-progress counters.

In `reconcile_snapshot_from_lineage` (service.rs:169-171), `active_bead_ids(&current_bead_runs)` is called where `current_bead_runs` is already the output of `latest_task_runs_per_bead`. Since `active_bead_ids` internally calls `latest_task_runs_per_bead` again, this performs a redundant O(n) deduplication pass that is guaranteed to be a no-op. Replace line 171 with a direct filter on `current_bead_runs` (the same logic `active_bead_ids` uses after its internal `latest_task_runs_per_bead` call) to avoid the wasted work and the misleading indirection that suggests the data hasn't already been collapsed.

The concurrency test's `LockAttemptSnapshotStore` (service.rs:710-768) consumes its channels via `Mutex<Option<T>>::take()`, meaning the signaling mechanism is single-use: only the first `with_milestone_write_lock` call will signal and wait; subsequent calls silently become no-ops. This is fine for the current test that calls it exactly once, but the type's name and structure (implementing the full `MilestoneSnapshotPort` trait) suggest it could be reused. Add a comment or assertion documenting that the store is designed for single-use lock coordination to prevent misuse in future tests.

Prevent duplicate completion journal events on terminal replays that backfill optional metadata. `update_task_run` explicitly supports replaying an already-finalized row and backfilling missing `plan_hash`/`outcome_detail` (`src/adapters/fs.rs:2425-2457`, `src/adapters/fs.rs:2798-2828`), but the completion event payload includes those optional fields (`src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:233-245`) and `append_event_if_missing` only deduplicates exact `details` matches (`src/adapters/fs.rs:2326-2341`). If the first completion write succeeded without `plan_hash`/`outcome_detail` and a later replay adds them, the same attempt gets a second `BeadCompleted`/`BeadFailed` journal entry instead of repairing the original event.

Preserve `Paused` during lineage reconciliation instead of forcing it back to `Ready`. `reconcile_snapshot_from_lineage` unconditionally rewrites any non-terminal milestone with no active bead and any prior task runs to `Ready` (`src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:213-216`), and `update_task_run` persists that reconciled snapshot on every completion/backfill (`src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:574-583`). Because `Paused` is a first-class lifecycle state (`src/contexts/milestone_record/model.rs:58-59`), replaying lineage on a paused milestone silently clears the operator-imposed pause.

Contract: final_review:reviewer
Role: Reviewer

# Final Review Proposals

You are a code reviewer. Review the changes in this project for correctness, safety, and robustness.

Run `git diff` to see all changes, then read the key implementation files end-to-end. For each issue found, cite specific files and line numbers.

Ignore style and cosmetics — only report real bugs, safety problems, correctness gaps, and significant maintainability risks.

## Project Prompt

# Task: Track bead-to-task run linkage inside milestone state

## Bead ID: ralph-burning-9ni.2.4

## Goal

Record which Ralph task/project/run attempted which bead, with enough detail to audit retries and outcomes later.

## Requirements

The `TaskRunEntry` model in `src/contexts/milestone_record/model.rs` must capture:
- milestone_id (currently implicit from storage path — should be explicit in the entry for self-contained queries)
- bead_id (already present)
- current task/project id (already present as `project_id`)
- run_id if available (MISSING — add this field)
- outcome summary (already present as `TaskRunOutcome`, but add optional `outcome_detail` for richer context)
- prompt version or plan hash if relevant (MISSING — add `plan_hash` field)

## Changes Required

1. **Extend `TaskRunEntry`** in `src/contexts/milestone_record/model.rs`:
   - Add `run_id: Option<String>` — the specific run ID within the project
   - Add `plan_hash: Option<String>` — plan version/hash at time of execution
   - Add `outcome_detail: Option<String>` — human-readable outcome summary

2. **Add `update_task_run` to `TaskRunLineagePort`** in `src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs`:
   - Allow updating an existing entry's outcome (controller needs this after each run)
   - Signature: update by bead_id + project_id, setting new outcome + finished_at

3. **Add `find_runs_for_bead` query** in service layer:
   - Filter task runs by bead_id to answer "what happened to bead X?"
   - Return all attempts in chronological order

4. **Implement FS adapter** for the new port method in `src/adapters/fs.rs`

5. **Update `record_bead_start` and `record_bead_completion`** to accept and pass through the new fields

6. **Add tests** for:
   - TaskRunEntry serialization with new fields
   - find_runs_for_bead query
   - update_task_run flow
   - Multiple retries for same bead are all visible

## Acceptance Criteria

- Milestone state can answer "what happened to bead X?" without reconstructing from unrelated artifacts
- Controller can update linkage after each run outcome
- All new fields are optional for backward compatibility with existing NDJSON data

## Non-Goals

- Controller state machine (covered by 8.1)
- Lifecycle transitions (covered by 2.5)
- Status aggregation queries (covered by 2.3)

## Dependencies (already closed)

- ralph-burning-9ni.2.2: Milestone store and service layer ✅

## Authoritative JSON Schema

The JSON schema below is authoritative. Return only JSON that conforms exactly to it.

```json
{
  "$schema": "http://json-schema.org/draft-07/schema#",
  "definitions": {
    "FinalReviewProposal": {
      "description": "A raw amendment proposal returned by a final-review reviewer.",
      "properties": {
        "body": {
          "description": "The free-form amendment body. This is canonicalized and deduplicated by the orchestration layer before voting.",
          "type": "string"
        },
        "rationale": {
          "description": "Optional reviewer-provided rationale for why this amendment matters.",
          "type": [
            "string",
            "null"
          ]
        }
      },
      "required": [
        "body"
      ],
      "type": "object"
    }
  },
  "description": "Payload returned by each final-review reviewer during proposal collection.",
  "properties": {
    "amendments": {
      "description": "Proposed amendments. An empty list means \"no amendments\".",
      "items": {
        "$ref": "#/definitions/FinalReviewProposal"
      },
      "type": "array"
    },
    "summary": {
      "description": "Summary of the reviewer pass.",
      "type": "string"
    }
  },
  "required": [
    "amendments",
    "summary"
  ],
  "title": "FinalReviewProposalPayload",
  "type": "object"
}
```

Return ONLY valid JSON matching the following schema:
{
  "$schema": "http://json-schema.org/draft-07/schema#",

details.body: Extract the duplicated journal details rendering logic into a single shared function. `CompletionJournalDetails::render()` in `src/adapters/fs.rs:2347` and `completion_event_details()` in `src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:239` produce the same comma-delimited format independently. If either is modified without the other, the journal repair path (`repairable_completion_event`) will silently fail to merge entries — falling through to append duplicates instead of backfilling. Consolidate into one function (e.g., on `TaskRunEntry` or a shared helper) called by both sites.

Guard against field values containing the `, ` delimiter in `CompletionJournalDetails::parse()` (src/adapters/fs.rs:2280). The `outcome_detail` field is free-form text and can plausibly contain commas followed by spaces. While the current `split_once(", detail=")` approach handles a trailing detail value correctly, the intermediate `split_required` calls for `project=`, `run=`, and `plan_hash=` would misparse if any of those values contained `, `. Add a brief code comment or debug_assert documenting the assumption that project_id/run_id/plan_hash are simple identifiers that never contain `, `.

`src/adapters/fs.rs:2813-2833` violates the port contract documented at `src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:107-109`. When `update_task_run` is called with a `run_id` that has no exact match, it falls back to any single runless running row without checking whether other running rows for the same `bead_id`/`project_id` are also present. In a mixed legacy/corrupt state like `[run_id=None, running] + [run_id=run-2, running]`, calling `update_task_run(..., run_id=run-3, ...)` currently finalizes the runless row and leaves `run-2` running, even though the request is ambiguous and should be rejected. That can rewrite the wrong attempt and mis-state lineage.

`src/contexts/milestone_record/model.rs:431-468` and `src/adapters/fs.rs:3013-3021` only collapse legacy start+terminal pairs; they do not deduplicate repeated terminal rows for the same attempt. As a result, `find_runs_for_bead` will report replayed completions as separate retries if the NDJSON already contains duplicate terminal entries for one run. I reproduced this with one running row plus two identical succeeded rows for the same `started_at`, and `find_runs_for_bead` returned 2 entries. That breaks the new "what happened to bead X?" audit query by overstating retries/outcomes.

Contract: final_review:reviewer
Role: Reviewer

# Final Review Proposals

You are a code reviewer. Review the changes in this project for correctness, safety, and robustness.

Run `git diff` to see all changes, then read the key implementation files end-to-end. For each issue found, cite specific files and line numbers.

Ignore style and cosmetics — only report real bugs, safety problems, correctness gaps, and significant maintainability risks.

## Project Prompt

# Task: Track bead-to-task run linkage inside milestone state

## Bead ID: ralph-burning-9ni.2.4

## Goal

Record which Ralph task/project/run attempted which bead, with enough detail to audit retries and outcomes later.

## Requirements

The `TaskRunEntry` model in `src/contexts/milestone_record/model.rs` must capture:
- milestone_id (currently implicit from storage path — should be explicit in the entry for self-contained queries)
- bead_id (already present)
- current task/project id (already present as `project_id`)
- run_id if available (MISSING — add this field)
- outcome summary (already present as `TaskRunOutcome`, but add optional `outcome_detail` for richer context)
- prompt version or plan hash if relevant (MISSING — add `plan_hash` field)

## Changes Required

1. **Extend `TaskRunEntry`** in `src/contexts/milestone_record/model.rs`:
   - Add `run_id: Option<String>` — the specific run ID within the project
   - Add `plan_hash: Option<String>` — plan version/hash at time of execution
   - Add `outcome_detail: Option<String>` — human-readable outcome summary

2. **Add `update_task_run` to `TaskRunLineagePort`** in `src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs`:
   - Allow updating an existing entry's outcome (controller needs this after each run)
   - Signature: update by bead_id + project_id, setting new outcome + finished_at

3. **Add `find_runs_for_bead` query** in service layer:
   - Filter task runs by bead_id to answer "what happened to bead X?"
   - Return all attempts in chronological order

4. **Implement FS adapter** for the new port method in `src/adapters/fs.rs`

5. **Update `record_bead_start` and `record_bead_completion`** to accept and pass through the new fields

6. **Add tests** for:
   - TaskRunEntry serialization with new fields
   - find_runs_for_bead query
   - update_task_run flow
   - Multiple retries for same bead are all visible

## Acceptance Criteria

- Milestone state can answer "what happened to bead X?" without reconstructing from unrelated artifacts
- Controller can update linkage after each run outcome
- All new fields are optional for backward compatibility with existing NDJSON data

## Non-Goals

- Controller state machine (covered by 8.1)
- Lifecycle transitions (covered by 2.5)
- Status aggregation queries (covered by 2.3)

## Dependencies (already closed)

- ralph-burning-9ni.2.2: Milestone store and service layer ✅

## Authoritative JSON Schema

The JSON schema below is authoritative. Return only JSON that conforms exactly to it.

```json
{
  "$schema": "http://json-schema.org/draft-07/schema#",
  "definitions": {
    "FinalReviewProposal": {
      "description": "A raw amendment proposal returned by a final-review reviewer.",
      "properties": {
        "body": {
          "description": "The free-form amendment body. This is canonicalized and deduplicated by the orchestration layer before voting.",
          "type": "string"
        },
        "rationale": {
          "description": "Optional reviewer-provided rationale for why this amendment matters.",
          "type": [
            "string",
            "null"
          ]
        }
      },
      "required": [
        "body"
      ],
      "type": "object"
    }
  },
  "description": "Payload returned by each final-review reviewer during proposal collection.",
  "properties": {
    "amendments": {
      "description": "Proposed amendments. An empty list means \"no amendments\".",
      "items": {
        "$ref": "#/definitions/FinalReviewProposal"
      },
      "type": "array"
    },
    "summary": {
      "description": "Summary of the reviewer pass.",
      "type": "string"
    }
  },
  "required": [
    "amendments",
    "summary"
  ],
  "title": "FinalReviewProposalPayload",
  "type": "object"
}
```

details.body: `src/contexts/milestone_record/model.rs:347-378` and `src/adapters/fs.rs:2280-2318` serialize completion details with a raw `", "`-delimited string, but the only protection against `project_id`/`run_id`/`plan_hash` containing that delimiter is `debug_assert!`. In release builds those checks disappear, and `ProjectId::new` / `RunId::new` still accept commas and spaces (`src/shared/domain.rs:804-838`). A legal id like `"foo, bar"` makes `CompletionJournalDetails::parse` fail, which means the repair path in `append_event_if_missing` (`src/adapters/fs.rs:2416-2493`) cannot merge or backfill replayed completion events. The result is duplicate completion journal rows instead of the intended idempotent repair whenever a replay adds missing `run_id`, `plan_hash`, or `outcome_detail`.

`FsTaskRunLineageStore::append_task_run` still writes whatever `TaskRunEntry` it is handed (`src/adapters/fs.rs:2682-2691`). Because `milestone_id` remains optional and `read_task_runs_from_path` only backfills it in memory after deserialization (`src/adapters/fs.rs:2525-2533`), any direct caller of the public append API can still create brand-new NDJSON rows with no explicit `milestone_id`. That violates the new self-contained-entry invariant introduced by this task and leaves on-disk lineage incomplete until some later rewrite happens to touch the row.

In the legacy repair fallback path (fs.rs:2449-2453), when an existing journal event has unparseable legacy details (starts with 'project=' but both JSON and legacy parsing fail), the code silently replaces the existing details wholesale with the requested details. If the merge path at line 2440-2446 returns None due to a conflict (e.g., different project_id or outcome between the misparsed legacy data and requested data), but the legacy data was actually just a formatting issue, the code falls through to the starts_with('project=') branch and overwrites without any conflict check. Consider logging a warning when this fallback fires so operators can audit whether data was lost.

`src/contexts/milestone_record/model.rs:415-447` and `src/adapters/fs.rs:2760-2774` let `record_task_run_start` reuse a lone runless open row even when another named run for the same bead/project is already open. In a mixed legacy state like `(run_id=None, outcome=running)` plus `(run_id="run-2", outcome=running)`, starting `run-3` returns `Ok(())` and rewrites the runless row to `run-3`, leaving two simultaneous open attempts for the same bead/project. I reproduced this with a small harness against the current code. That corrupts lineage attribution instead of surfacing the state as ambiguous; the start path should reject this mixed-open case the same way `update_task_run` already does.

`src/adapters/fs.rs:2648-2657` and `src/adapters/fs.rs:3015-3020` silently ignore a conflicting `plan_hash` once a running row already has any hash. I reproduced a normal start/completion flow where `record_bead_start(..., plan_hash="plan-v1")` is followed by `record_bead_completion(..., plan_hash="plan-v2")`; the call succeeds, but the persisted `TaskRunEntry` still reports `plan_hash="plan-v1"`. Because the whole point of this task is to audit which plan/run attempted a bead, returning success while dropping the caller's newer hash produces incorrect milestone lineage data. The update path should either reject conflicting hashes or explicitly overwrite them.

`src/contexts/milestone_record/model.rs:431-442` and `src/adapters/fs.rs:2815-2832` unconditionally reuse a sole legacy running row (`run_id == None`) when a new start arrives with `run_id = Some(...)`. That is safe for idempotent repair, but it is wrong for a real retry after upgrade: a stale pre-upgrade open row and a genuinely new run get merged into one attempt, because the old row is backfilled instead of appending a second `TaskRunEntry`. This loses retry history and violates the requirement that bead attempts remain auditable by run.

`src/contexts/milestone_record/model.rs:302-310` plus `src/contexts/milestone_record/model.rs:465-490` make the no-`run_id` path depend on `started_at` uniqueness. Distinct attempts in the same `project_id` are considered the same attempt whenever their timestamps match, and terminal rows with the same outcome are then collapsed into one history entry. Because `run_id` is explicitly optional for backward compatibility, this means two retries can disappear from `find_runs_for_bead` if the caller reuses or truncates timestamps, which breaks the acceptance criterion that multiple retries remain visible.

Fix same-timestamp retry ordering in `latest_task_runs_per_bead`. In `src/contexts/milestone_record/model.rs:379-389`, `compare_task_run_recency` treats a terminal row as newer than a later retry whenever both attempts share the same `started_at`, because it compares `finished_at` before anything else. `reconcile_snapshot_from_lineage` in `src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:163-204` uses that result to derive `active_bead` and progress, so a retry that starts at the same timestamp as an earlier failed/skipped attempt can leave the milestone in `ready` with `in_progress_beads=0` even though a new run is active. This needs a deterministic tie-break that preserves the newest attempt when timestamps collide, plus a regression test covering same-timestamp retries affecting snapshot state.

Add a recovery path for start retries when `run_id` is absent. `record_bead_start` writes lineage/snapshot before it appends the `BeadStarted` journal event (`src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:428-451`). If that final append fails, the retry logic only reuses the existing running row when a runless caller supplies the exact original `started_at` (`src/contexts/milestone_record/model.rs:435-442`); otherwise `record_task_run_start` falls through to the "already active" error (`src/adapters/fs.rs:2815-2858`). For legacy callers still passing `run_id=None`, a transient journal failure leaves the start operation unrecoverable by a normal retry. The start matching/backfill logic should be widened so runless callers can repair the partial write, and this needs a regression test.

TaskRunEntry.milestone_id is marked Option<String> with #[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")] (model.rs:277-278), but the acceptance criteria states: "New fields (run_id, plan_hash, outcome_detail, milestone_id) should be required (non-optional) where they are known at write time." milestone_id is always known at write time — it's passed as a parameter to every write path. Make milestone_id a required String field. This also removes the need for backfill-on-read in FsTaskRunLineageStore::read_task_runs_from_path (fs.rs:2556-2558) and the canonicalize_entry_for_write method that patches it up.

Remove CompletionJournalDetails::parse_legacy (fs.rs:2289-2321) and the legacy-format branch in repairable_completion_event (fs.rs:2449-2459) that handles comma-delimited 'project=..., outcome=...' journal details. The acceptance criteria states: 'Remove any legacy parsing, backfill-on-read, or repair paths that only exist to handle a hypothetical migration from an older schema.' Since there is no existing milestone data on disk, the only journal details format is the structured JSON written by render_completion_journal_details. The parse_legacy path adds dead code and masks serialization bugs.

Remove the backward-compat deserialization test task_run_entry_backward_compat_without_new_fields (service.rs:1232-1245) and the legacy-backfill test read_task_runs_backfills_milestone_id_for_legacy_entries (service.rs:1247-1282). These tests validate migration behavior from an older schema that the acceptance criteria says does not exist. Also remove progress_deserialization_defaults_skipped_beads (model.rs test) if skipped_beads is made a required field (removing its #[serde(default)] annotation at model.rs:204).

Fix start-attempt matching so it distinguishes a real retry from an idempotent replay. `find_matching_running_task_run` ignores its `started_at` parameter entirely (`src/contexts/milestone_record/model.rs:416-442`), and `record_task_run_start` then either reuses any sole runless open row or appends a second row when the retry arrives with a newly known `run_id` (`src/adapters/fs.rs:2815-2856`). That makes the audit trail wrong in both directions: a genuine second retry without `run_id` is silently collapsed into the first attempt, while a replay of the same logical attempt that now has a `run_id` leaves behind a phantom `Running` row forever. The current behavior is even codified in `src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:3179-3235`.

In `record_task_run_start` (fs.rs ~line 2770-2797), when `find_matching_running_task_run` returns None but there IS exactly one matching running entry (len==1, different started_at), a new running entry is appended without finalizing the old one. This creates two open runless entries for the same bead/project. Subsequently calling `update_task_run` with `run_id=None` will hit the `open_matches.len() > 1` branch and fail with 'ambiguous task run update'. The test `runless_retry_with_new_started_at_appends_new_attempt` validates the start behavior but does not test that the resulting state is completable. Either: (a) auto-fail the old running entry when creating a new attempt (set outcome=Failed, finished_at=started_at of new attempt), or (b) add started_at as a disambiguator to update_task_run, or (c) add a test proving that callers are expected to always supply run_id when multiple attempts exist and document this constraint.

`src/adapters/fs.rs:2103-2111` and `src/adapters/fs.rs:2254-2264` create `milestones/<id>/` just to take the per-milestone lock, before any existence check runs. Any typoed `update_status`/`persist_plan`/`record_bead_start`/`update_task_run` call will therefore leave behind an empty milestone directory plus `mutation.lock`; after that, `FsMilestoneStore::milestone_exists` (`src/adapters/fs.rs:2131-2142`) treats the ID as corrupt, so `create_milestone` can no longer create that milestone without manual cleanup.

`read_task_runs_from_path` never verifies that each deserialized `TaskRunEntry.milestone_id` matches the milestone file being read (`src/adapters/fs.rs:2478-2500`). Downstream code then trusts those rows for both snapshot reconciliation (`src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:163-223`) and the new bead query (`src/adapters/fs.rs:3056-3073`), so a misfiled row inside `milestones/A/task-runs.ndjson` will change A's active bead/progress or appear in A's audit results instead of surfacing corruption.

MilestoneId::new() in model.rs should reject dot-prefixed names (e.g., `.locks`, `.hidden`) to prevent collision with the new MILESTONE_LOCKS_DIR (`.locks`) directory. Currently, creating a milestone with id `.locks` would place its data directory at `milestones/.locks/`, which is the same path used for lock files of all other milestones. Add a validation rule like `normalized.starts_with('.')` to the existing rejection checks in MilestoneId::new().

Tighten `FsTaskRunLineageStore::update_task_run` so a completion with `run_id` only finalizes an exact matching attempt. Today `src/adapters/fs.rs:2894-2928` falls back to the lone unfinished runless row when no exact `run_id` match exists, and `record_bead_completion` no longer supplies `started_at` to disambiguate (`src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:457-492`). That means a completion for a brand-new named retry can be written onto an older open attempt instead of failing fast, which corrupts the bead audit trail.

Handle stale legacy/open attempts when starting a new named retry. In `src/adapters/fs.rs:2827-2836`, superseding only happens when the new start is itself runless; if a new explicit `run_id` starts after an older runless row, the old row is left permanently `running`. `find_runs_for_bead` then returns contradictory history for the same bead (`src/adapters/fs.rs:3111-3127`), e.g. one attempt still `running` even after a later retry already `succeeded`. Either mark the older row as superseded at start time or reject the new start until the old row is resolved.

In MilestoneId::new (model.rs:25), the added `normalized.starts_with('.')` check makes the subsequent `normalized == "."` and `normalized == ".."` conditions dead code — they can never be reached since both "." and ".." start with '.'. Remove the two now-redundant equality checks to keep the validation predicate minimal and avoid confusion about whether there's a semantic difference between the conditions.

`src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:464-490` drops the caller-provided `started_at`, and `src/adapters/fs.rs:2982-3009` resolves runless completions only by `(bead_id, project_id)`. That breaks the supported `run_id = None` retry path when the same project is reused: after the second runless attempt is finalized, any replay of `record_bead_completion` for that attempt fails with `ambiguous task run update ... provide run_id to disambiguate`, so a partial snapshot/journal write can no longer be repaired. `started_at` needs to be used to pick the attempt, or the API needs to forbid same-project runless retries entirely.

`src/adapters/fs.rs:2770-2845` allows starting the same bead again as long as the other active row is for the same `bead_id`; it only rejects a different active bead. `src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:163-204` then rebuilds snapshot state from `latest_task_runs_per_bead`, which keeps only the newest attempt per bead. The result is that if `bead-1/project-1/run-1` is still `Running`, `bead-1/project-2/run-2` can start, and once `run-2` reaches a terminal state the milestone snapshot moves to `ready` with `in_progress_beads = 0` even though `task-runs.ndjson` still contains the older running attempt. Start logic needs to reject or explicitly supersede any prior running attempt for the same bead, not only same-project legacy rows.

Contract: final_review:reviewer
Role: Reviewer

# Final Review Proposals

You are a code reviewer. Review the changes in this project for correctness, safety, and robustness.

Run `git diff` to see all changes, then read the key implementation files end-to-end. For each issue found, cite specific files and line numbers.

Ignore style and cosmetics — only report real bugs, safety problems, correctness gaps, and significant maintainability risks.

## Project Prompt

# Task: Track bead-to-task run linkage inside milestone state

## Bead ID: ralph-burning-9ni.2.4

## Goal

Record which Ralph task/project/run attempted which bead, with enough detail to audit retries and outcomes later.

## Requirements

The `TaskRunEntry` model in `src/contexts/milestone_record/model.rs` must capture:
- milestone_id (currently implicit from storage path — should be explicit in the entry for self-contained queries)
- bead_id (already present)
- current task/project id (already present as `project_id`)
- run_id if available (MISSING — add this field)
- outcome summary (already present as `TaskRunOutcome`, but add optional `outcome_detail` for richer context)
- prompt version or plan hash if relevant (MISSING — add `plan_hash` field)

## Changes Required

1. **Extend `TaskRunEntry`** in `src/contexts/milestone_record/model.rs`:
   - Add `run_id: Option<String>` — the specific run ID within the project
   - Add `plan_hash: Option<String>` — plan version/hash at time of execution
   - Add `outcome_detail: Option<String>` — human-readable outcome summary

2. **Add `update_task_run` to `TaskRunLineagePort`** in `src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs`:
   - Allow updating an existing entry's outcome (controller needs this after each run)
   - Signature: update by bead_id + project_id, setting new outcome + finished_at

3. **Add `find_runs_for_bead` query** in service layer:
   - Filter task runs by bead_id to answer "what happened to bead X?"
   - Return all attempts in chronological order

4. **Implement FS adapter** for the new port method in `src/adapters/fs.rs`

5. **Update `record_bead_start` and `record_bead_completion`** to accept and pass through the new fields

6. **Add tests** for:
   - TaskRunEntry serialization with new fields
   - find_runs_for_bead query
   - update_task_run flow
   - Multiple retries for same bead are all visible

## Acceptance Criteria

- Milestone state can answer "what happened to bead X?" without reconstructing from unrelated artifacts
- Controller can update linkage after each run outcome
- No backward compatibility with old NDJSON schemas is required — there is no existing milestone data on disk. New fields (run_id, plan_hash, outcome_detail, milestone_id) should be required (non-optional) where they are known at write time. Remove any legacy parsing, backfill-on-read, or repair paths that only exist to handle a hypothetical migration from an older schema.

## Non-Goals

- Controller state machine (covered by 8.1)
- Lifecycle transitions (covered by 2.5)
- Status aggregation queries (covered by 2.3)

## Dependencies (already closed)

- ralph-burning-9ni.2.2: Milestone store and service layer ✅

## Authoritative JSON Schema

The JSON schema below is authoritative. Return only JSON that conforms exactly to it.

```json
{
  "$schema": "http://json-schema.org/draft-07/schema#",
  "definitions": {
    "FinalReviewProposal": {
      "description": "A raw amendment proposal returned by a final-review reviewer.",
      "properties": {
        "body": {
          "description": "The free-form amendment body. This is canonicalized and deduplicated by the orchestration layer before voting.",
          "type": "string"
        },
        "rationale": {
          "description": "Optional reviewer-provided rationale for why this amendment matters.",
          "type": [
            "string",
            "null"
          ]
        }
      },
      "required": [
        "body"
      ],
      "type": "object"
    }
  },
  "description": "Payload returned by each final-review reviewer during proposal collection.",
  "properties": {
    "amendments": {
      "description": "Proposed amendments. An empty list means \"no amendments\".",
      "items": {
        "$ref": "#/definitions/FinalReviewProposal"
      },
      "type": "array"
    },
    "summary": {
      "description": "Summary of the reviewer pass.",
      "type": "string"
    }
  },
```

details.body: src/adapters/fs.rs:2777-2839 and src/contexts/milestone_record/model.rs:410-445 still treat a runless `record_bead_start` replay as a brand-new attempt once the original row has already been finalized. The finalized-attempt guard only runs when `run_id` is `Some`, and `find_matching_running_task_run` only looks at non-terminal rows. Replaying the original start after a runless completion therefore appends a second `Running` row; after snapshot reconciliation in src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:164-224, the milestone flips back to `Active` even though the bead already finished.

In `matching_finalized_task_runs` (model.rs:448-480), the `started_at` fallback path does not exclude entries whose `run_id` is `Some(different_id)`. When a caller provides `run_id = Some("run-3")` and the DB has a finalized entry with `run_id = Some("run-2")` at the same `started_at`, the fallback returns that entry — blocking a legitimate new run with a misleading "already finalized" error. The fallback filter (line 476-479) should additionally require `entry.run_id.is_none()` when `run_id` was provided, so it only catches the runless-to-named upgrade case the tests intend to cover, not a collision between two distinct named runs that happen to share a timestamp.

`src/adapters/fs.rs:2295-2322` and `src/adapters/fs.rs:2390-2469` merge completion journal events based only on `timestamp + event_type + bead_id` plus loosely compatible details. If two different attempts for the same bead/project complete at the same timestamp and at least one lacks a `run_id`, `append_event_if_missing` will repair/overwrite the first event instead of appending a second one. Tighten the dedupe key to a real attempt identity (for example `run_id`, or `started_at` when that is the explicit identifier) or drop this repair path.

Fix the `BeadStarted` journal dedupe collision introduced by allowing distinct named runs to share a `started_at`. `src/contexts/milestone_record/model.rs:469-482` now lets `run-3` start even when `run-2` already finalized at the same timestamp, but `src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:448-452` still records the start event as only `project={project_id}`. Because `src/adapters/fs.rs:2452-2457` treats `(timestamp, event_type, bead_id, details)` as the dedupe key, two same-project starts at the same timestamp collapse into one journal row and the later retry disappears from the milestone audit trail. Include `run_id`/`plan_hash` in `BeadStarted` details or otherwise widen the dedupe key for start events.
